title: v0.1 Alpha Release
description: Shipped across the v0.1.x releases. Gate for making the repo public and sharing with coworkers. Language surface is honest — named arguments (#56), agents nest in modules (#63), types usable from agents (#70), enum variant construction completeness (#96), capability checks cover test blocks (#104) — schedule handlers fire on their own (#71), agent events survive crashes (#72), every spec example compiles or is marked Planned (#77), the first-five-minutes DX holds up (assertion failures say why #105, skein new git-inits #106, zsh completion #101, MCP compile_check tells the truth #109), and releases/badges/versioned docs are automated for public consumption (#100).

title: v0.2 Beta Release
description: Shipped as v0.2.0. Post-alpha hardening, informed by alpha feedback: replay backend injection (#73), the capability-parameter surface decision (#69) and parameter-scoped enforcement for process/timer/event.log (#57), process.spawn task bodies (#74), enum value-level exhaustiveness warnings (#76), local LLM backends for dev via OpenAI-compatible servers + skein.toml profiles (#107), LSP code actions from fix_hint/fix_code (#108), and the Int string-interpolation fix (#114).

title: v1.0.0-rc Release
description: The gate for tagging v1.0.0-rc.1 — output of the 2026-06-11 release-readiness pass. Shipped: every item fixed and v1.0.0-rc.1 tagged 2026-06-12. NOTE (2026-06-15 / 2026-06-19 roadmap resets): rc.1 was tagged prematurely. The 2026-06-14/15 dogfooding audit (skein-testing + FablePool ports) and the 2026-06-19 source-verified soundness/contract audit reset the GA picture — v1.0.0 is no longer an imminent RC promotion. The next release is the development milestone v0.4.0, not another RC. See docs/ROADMAP.md.

title: v0.4.0 — Truth & Soundness
description: Shipped as v0.4.0 (released 2026-07-02 via PR #328). First reset wave (1.0 blockers). Wave A — Truth reset & surface cuts: resume de-reserved (#301), E0028/E0029 registry rows (#312), `!`/`?` standardization + get!/put! deletion (#268), tool policy blocks cut + supervisor surface pinned (#319), expression-termination grammar (#318), flakes #271/#272 closed. Wave B — Analyzer/codegen soundness: COMPLETE (B1–B6 / #290–#295, source-verified and adversarially probed by the 2026-07-02 sanity check — docs/audits/2026-07-post-wave-b-sanity-check.md); all residue (#309–#311, #313) closed by the close-out sweep (PR #327).

title: v0.5.0 — Runtime Contract & Dogfood
description: Shipped as v0.5.0 (released 2026-07-02 via PR #330 — all 10 issues, one commit each). Second reset wave (1.0 blockers), closing the runtime-contract drift found by the 2026-06-19 audit. Wave C: C1 authoritative effect-ABI registry (`Skein.EffectABI` drives analyzer/codegen/spec §6/runtime ABI-matrix; drift is a CI failure — #296); C2 one structured-error ABI (every effect error matchable from Skein; variant registry frozen; `LiveEffectError` uncatchability frozen — #297); C3 one recursive schema engine across `req.json[T]`/`llm.json[T]`/tool input+output (#298); C4 remainder (`llm.embed` resolves past model providers; `given` kept + specified — #279); C5 typed store tables (`store.table("games", Game)` required, E0043; schema-checked ETS writes — #255); C6 EventStore SQLite persistence on the ordinary append path (default-on under `skein run` — #299); #325 real OTP supervision from `supervisor` declarations. Wave D (#262): both external ports migrated and passing; checked-in pinned reductions run in every `mix test` and CI executes upstream clones at the pins; every complete docs/spec `skein` fence compiles; complete-diagnostic negative corpus; B4 generator widened (#314); analyzer factoring (#315). Minimal canonical substrate stayed out per #300 → Alternative B.

title: v1.0.0-rc.5 — True release candidate
description: The next active dev gate (Wave F). Renamed from v1.0.0-rc.2 on 2026-07-02: tags v1.0.0-rc.2–rc.4 already exist from the pre-reset June train, so the next RC tag is v1.0.0-rc.5. Prerequisites are met — v0.4.0 and v0.5.0 both shipped 2026-07-02 and the dogfood conformance gate executes both external ports at their pins on every PR (#300 resolved as B: no canonical-substrate milestone). Contents: the Wave F freeze itself (#332 — verify + declare every frozen surface with executable gates: grammar/keywords, diagnostics, effect ABI + error shapes, schema derivation vectors, CLI/JSON/config, compiled-metadata classes, EventStore persisted vectors, dogfood pins), the agent-writability benchmark reporting in release-readiness (#320), and live-backend blocker verification (#334). No feature work in this milestone — only freeze verification, blocker fixes, docs corrections, migration fixes, and release mechanics. Run the exact release-readiness workflow used for GA; soak the same candidate before promoting to v1.0.0.

title: v1.0.0 Release
description: The GA tag — NOT imminent (reset 2026-06-15, re-baselined 2026-06-19). Freeze only after all preceding contracts are executable and green. GA is a sound, honest, deterministic, dogfood-proven core: (1) sound analyzer/codegen/runtime (no analyzer-success crash/miscompute; no `:unknown` at public boundaries; true `?`; tagged records/Option); (2) typed/honest capabilities & effects with one effect-ABI matrix and one structured-error ABI; (3) scenario-scoped capability environments (analyzer + runtime); (4) deterministic/replayable scenario/golden testing; (5) one recursive schema engine across request/LLM/tool-in/tool-out; (6) honest store + EventStore contracts; (7) dogfood conformance across Skein, skein-testing, and FablePool-skein as a continuous gate. (The former canonical bytes/hash substrate condition resolved OUT of 1.0 on 2026-07-02: #300 closed as Alternative B — substrate lives in v1.1.) Items 1–7 all landed with v0.4.0 + v0.5.0 (shipped 2026-07-02); what remains before GA is the Wave F freeze + RC soak. Tags once v1.0.0-rc.5 has soaked and every blocker is closed. Application/memory policy (provenance, lineage, grants, redaction) stays OUT of the language.

title: v1.1: Hardening & Language
description: Post-1.0 language + effect work intentionally cut from the minimal 1.0 surface (default home for deferred items). Closures/anonymous functions (#248), record update (#251), module constants (#249), enum variant payloads (#247), `via`/`via Module` only if a need survives now that scenario capability environments shipped (v0.4.0/v0.5.0), behavioural stateful stubs, general effect-provider interfaces, content-addressed store tables (#333 — successor to the content half of #255, which C5 consumed), effectful crypto — secure RNG/keygen/signing (#257), `llm.rerank` (#145), docs/spec drift guards (#202 — the compile-every-fence subset already landed in Wave D), and the LSP structural-navigation work (#240). Home for the canonical-bytes/hash/encoding substrate (#245/#256/#246/#250) — #300 resolved 2026-07-02 as Alternative B, so these stay here. Everything here is additive under the STABILITY policy (new grammar/stdlib/effect surface lands in minors — nothing deferred becomes a breaking change later). A specific item promotes to 1.0 only when a checked-in executable conformance program proves it indispensable.

title: v1.2: Interop & Agent Workflows
description: Bigger design efforts after the hardening wave: Erlang/Elixir FFI via extern (#141, explicitly cut from 1.0 — the crypto route is the v1.1 stdlib/effect work (#245/#257), never general FFI), human-in-the-loop approval workflows with suspend before sensitive tool calls (#144), the browser-based trace viewer (#143), the Raxol TUI for an interactive CLI (#171, separately gated from the framework-neutral CLI work), and native structural search/codemod (#241).

title: Future: Platform
description: Deliberately deferred platform bets, parked until the core language and runtime stabilize: hot code upgrades (#142), the managed deployment platform (#148), and the tools/connectors marketplace (#149). Re-scope each from scratch when pulled into active work.
